artifact_scope.note: This inventory describes the current Android code path, Firebase Authentication usage, RC3 Crashlytics boundary, and RC4 Firebase Cloud Messaging source integration. Crashlytics collection and FCM auto-init are disabled by default. RC4 participant token registration and notification permission prompting remain source-controlled disabled; only an exact-source synthetic managed canary may explicitly obtain a test token and deliver bounded test pushes.

sdk_inventory.active_use: Phone authentication only when all protected pilot bindings are configured.
automatic_processing: IP address for security and abuse prevention
Firebase user agent/device metadata and SDK/platform version information
usage_dependent_processing: Play Integrity or anti-abuse tokens used by the phone-auth flow when applicable
sdk_inventory.sharing_assessment: service-provider-processing-not-declared-as-third-party-sharing-under-current-model; final Play form must be rechecked against the exact SDK/version and contracts

sdk_inventory.active_use: RC3 managed synthetic/debug canary proven; normal preauthorization and production-participant collection are not authorized.
synthetic_canary_processing: fixed synthetic crash or ANR diagnostics
app/device/OS metadata generated by Crashlytics for the synthetic canary
prohibited_custom_context: provider evidence bytes or reviewer-private notes
sdk_inventory.sharing_assessment: synthetic-canary-only under RC3; final Play/Data Safety mapping must be revalidated before any participant or production telemetry is authorized

sdk_inventory.active_use: RC4 source-integrated with firebase_messaging_auto_init_enabled=false, participant registration disabled, and managed synthetic foreground/background canary pending.
synthetic_canary_processing: temporary Firebase registration token on the managed synthetic test device
Firebase installation/device identifiers required by FCM for the synthetic test delivery
bounded synthetic routing fields: delivery ID, source SHA and foreground/background phase
network/device metadata processed by Firebase for message delivery
sdk_inventory.token_handling: The synthetic token is read from app-private storage, masked, transferred through a one-run Secret Manager secret, used only by the backend FCM HTTP v1 canary job, excluded from logs/artifacts/API responses, and deleted after the run. Participant token registration remains disabled.
sdk_inventory.sharing_assessment: synthetic-managed-canary-only during RC4; final Play/Data Safety mapping and consent/notification controls must be revalidated before participant or production push is authorized

play_data_types.collection_condition: Only when protected Firebase phone authentication is enabled and the invited user starts verification.
play_data_types.required_or_optional: required for the current approved phone-auth sign-in method
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Firebase/provider and DIREKT account lifecycle controls apply. Final deletion fulfillment, legal-hold rules and public deletion request path remain release blockers pending qualified review and implementation evidence.

play_data_types.play_category: Device or other IDs / User IDs
play_data_types.collection_condition: Firebase creates an authentication user ID; DIREKT exchanges it into server-authoritative identity/session identifiers and stores only a protected digest of the external subject server-side.
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Session identifiers expire/revoke; account identity retention/deletion follows the approved rights and legal schedule, which is not yet final for real production data.

play_data_types.play_category: Device or other IDs / Device metadata and network identifiers
play_data_types.collection_condition: Firebase Authentication automatically processes service metadata when authentication is used. FCM auto-init is disabled in the normal release path; RC4's exact-source synthetic canary may explicitly create a temporary registration token/device identifier solely for managed test delivery.
play_data_types.required_or_optional: automatic for approved Firebase Authentication use; synthetic-only and temporary for RC4 FCM proof while participant push remains disabled
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Provider-controlled service retention applies. RC4 synthetic token material is temporary and deleted from DIREKT-managed storage after the run. Final participant-push form mapping must be revalidated against the exact shipped Firebase SDK and policy before activation.

play_data_types.play_category: App activity / Consent and authentication events
play_data_types.collection_condition: DIREKT records the approved notice version, affirmative acceptance and bounded authentication/session security metadata during successful admission.
play_data_types.required_or_optional: required for controlled admission and accountability
play_data_types.retention_and_deletion: Bounded consent/audit history is retained according to the approved policy/legal schedule; final real-data schedule remains subject to qualified review.

not_collected_by_current_android_release: broad photos or media library access
analytics events through an analytics SDK
participant or production crash/ANR telemetry while Crashlytics automatic collection remains default-off
participant FCM registration tokens or participant push-delivery metadata while FCM auto-init and participant registration remain disabled
real provider evidence bytes through an Android upload flow
real enquiry/review/complaint text through the current synthetic preview interaction surfaces

future_or_blocked_product_data.data: provider profile, evidence, location, enquiries, contact handoff, reviews, complaints, subscription/payment records
future_or_blocked_product_data.status: backend contracts exist but the current Android production-capable release path must not declare these as active collection until the exact release candidate actually transmits them and the approved privacy/legal basis is live

Crashlytics SDK is source-integrated with automatic collection disabled; RC3 managed synthetic proof does not authorize participant/production collection. A separate reviewed privacy/data-use decision and exact release-candidate revalidation are required before enabling it.

future_or_blocked_product_data.data: participant FCM registration tokens and push delivery metadata
future_or_blocked_product_data.status: Firebase Messaging is source-integrated with auto-init disabled and participant registration disabled. RC4 managed synthetic proof does not authorize participant/production push. Separate consent/privacy/release authorization and exact release-candidate revalidation are required before activation.

security_answers_candidate.request_account_deletion_blocker: Google Play requires an in-app path and public web request route when account creation is supported. DIREKT has sign-out/session revocation but the final account deletion request/fulfillment workflow and public route are not yet production-approved.
security_answers_candidate.notes: Do not claim optional Play security badges, deletion support, participant crash telemetry, or participant push delivery until evidence and authorization exist.

submission_boundary: The Play developer is responsible for the final declaration. Revalidate this inventory against the exact release candidate, merged manifest, SDK versions, backend behavior, approved privacy policy, telemetry/messaging configuration and live account-deletion flow before submitting any closed/open/production release.
